element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial zone implicitly through opening villages
Players learn NPCs offer clues or deliberate lies; optional tutorial through merchant encounters
Reinforced through merchant transactions in villages
Passive accumulation; no explicit notification in final game (unused level-up messages exist in ROM)
Daytime enemies weaker; nighttime enemies stronger but drop more hearts. Villages become populated by day, zombies by night
Purchased from merchants; unlock hidden areas and provide tactical options
Five mansions house Dracula's body parts; hazardous indoor environments

element_introduction_order.element: Dracula's body parts as collectible items
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Parts found in mansions (e.g., Dracula's Rib blocks projectiles); required for final boss encounter
Essential quest item alongside body parts for accessing Dracula's Castle

element_introduction_order.element: Castle Dracula ruins blockade and final boss
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Final confrontation; three possible endings based on completion time (8 days for best ending)

typical_relief_triggers: Entering villages during day (NPCs available, safe zone)
typical_tension_triggers: Nightfall transition (enemies strengthen, NPCs replace with zombies)
Backtracking through dark areas for missed items
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical day/night structure creates macro-level rhythm; exploration-driven pacing allows player-controlled tension through risk/reward decisions (night farming for hearts vs. safety of daylight travel). Non-linear world enables player agency in sequencing challenge encounters.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Mansions accessible via exploration; villages provide safe hub points during day; world map encourages revisiting areas under different conditions (day vs. night) revealing new enemy patterns and opportunities
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Holy Water disintegrates hidden walls to reveal concealed items; vertical exploration within mansions yields collectibles; night-time encourages backtracking to known areas for increased heart drops

difficulty_curve.early_game: Weak daytime enemies introduce combat safely; village hubs provide merchant safety nets; low cost items accessible quickly
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction to mansion exploration with moderate platforming hazards; day/night cycle begins presenting tactical choices (farm hearts at night vs. explore safely by day); experience system grants passive stat growth
difficulty_curve.late_game: Five mansions present escalating environmental challenges; body part collection gates progression toward final castle; completion window (8-day time limit for best ending) creates tension as deadline approaches
notable_spikes: Transition to first mansion (shift from open-world exploration to contained platforming)
Nighttime enemy encounters in outdoor areas (steep stat boost to enemies)
Castle Dracula approach (final gauntlet requiring all collected items)

notable_level_patterns.description: Safe daytime zones where NPCs offer dialogue (clues or lies), merchants sell items for hearts. Becomes populated by zombies at night, removing social safety but enabling resource farming.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any village location (unnamed in sources; generic throughout game)

notable_level_patterns.description: Five distinct mansion structures requiring exploration to locate Dracula's body parts. Indoor platforming hazards and contained vertical/horizontal navigation contrast with open-world exploration.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Five mansions (specific names not documented in sources)

notable_level_patterns.description: Same outdoor spaces present different challenge and reward profiles based on time of day. Daytime: safe travel, weak enemies, no NPC dialogue. Nighttime: dangerous enemies, higher heart drops, NPC replacement by zombies.
